name: The Noble Pawnbroker Inc.
description_short: Family-owned pawnbroker in Noble, Oklahoma offering collateral-based loans, firearms sales, jewelry, and a layaway program. Licensed and regulated by the state.
description_long:
The Noble Pawnbroker is a family-owned and operated pawnshop located in Noble, Oklahoma, established in May 2019. The business was taken over by Nick and Marisa Parker, who returned to their hometown after five years of missionary work in New Zealand. The founders emphasize a mission-driven approach, naming the business after the word "noble"—meaning moral, honest, brave, and kind—as a reflection of their customer service philosophy and commitment to serving the community with respect and friendliness.

The company offers traditional pawnshop services including short-term collateral-based loans (60-day terms per Oklahoma state law), item renewals, and the sale of pawned merchandise. They specialize in firearms (with federal licensing), jewelry, Benchmade knives, and general merchandise. A notable offering is their 10/10/10 layaway program for guns, gold, and Benchmade knives: 10% down, 10 months to pay, with a 10% monthly premium. They also list inventory on third-party platforms including GunBroker.com and Craigslist.com for broader reach.

The Noble Pawnbroker distinguishes itself through local, human-based valuation rather than computerized assessments. Management explicitly states they aim to "loan you top dollar on your item at an amount you are able to pay back." The business is licensed through Oklahoma's Office of Consumer Credit and subject to regular audits. They voluntarily submit inventory to law enforcement databases to prevent stolen merchandise (claiming less than 1% of items are identified as stolen) and require photo identification for all pawn and sale transactions.

As a pawnshop, this business serves customers needing quick cash access secured by collateral, typically in amounts determined by item value and condition. The company operates transparently about regulatory requirements (Patriot Act, Truth in Lending Act, federal firearms laws) and maintains standard pawnshop operational limitations, including the 60-day loan term mandated by Oklahoma law.
pros: Family-owned and mission-driven with founder community roots in Noble, Oklahoma since childhood
Loan valuations based on experienced management judgment rather than automated computer assessments
Voluntarily submits inventory to law enforcement databases to prevent stolen merchandise sales
Specialized layaway program (10/10/10) for guns, gold, and Benchmade knives—lowest payment option for these items
Authorized Benchmale dealer; explicitly advertises "best deal on Benchmade anywhere"
Multi-channel sales including in-store, GunBroker.com, and Craigslist for diverse inventory reach
Licensed through Oklahoma Office of Consumer Credit with regular audits and federal compliance framework
Requires photo identification for all pawns/sales to prevent theft and fraud
cons: Standard 60-day pawn loan term with no longer grace periods (Oklahoma state law limitation)
Renewal requires paying all accrued interest, which can increase total cost of borrowing significantly over time
Limited geographic service area (Noble, Oklahoma only); no online loan processing despite e-commerce presence
Loan amounts determined by management assessment of resale value, not item replacement value, potentially reducing offered amounts
Website content appears incomplete (FAQ section cuts off mid-sentence), suggesting outdated or poorly maintained online presence
best_for: Individuals in central Oklahoma needing quick cash against firearms, jewelry, or valuable personal items
Gun and knife enthusiasts seeking to purchase Benchmade knives or firearms at discounted prices via layaway
Borrowers preferring human-based item valuations over algorithmic assessments
Customers seeking transparent, regulation-compliant pawnshop services in a faith-based business environment
diagnosis: The Noble Pawnbroker is best for residents of Noble, Oklahoma and surrounding areas who need immediate cash access by pledging firearms, jewelry, or valuables, or who want to purchase used guns and knives at discounted prices. The main caveat is that pawn loans are inherently expensive relative to traditional lending (60-day terms plus interest, with renewals adding further costs), making them suitable only for short-term liquidity needs rather than long-term credit solutions.
services: 60-day collateral-based pawn loans on firearms, jewelry, electronics, and personal property
Pawn loan renewals (extending loan term by paying accrued interest)
10/10/10 layaway program (10% down, 10 months to pay, 10% monthly premium) for guns, gold, and Benchmade knives
Firearms sales (long-guns and handguns) with federal background checks and age verification
Benchmale knife sales as authorized dealer
Gold and jewelry buying and sales
Online inventory listings via GunBroker.com for firearms and shooting sports items
Online inventory listings via Craigslist for large items (vehicles, trailers, lawn mowers)
Customer identification and verification for regulatory compliance
